title: An uninitialized proxy can be seized by the first external caller
reason: This deployment is not a proxy (`_etherscan_meta.json` shows `proxy: 0`), and the constructor calls `_disableInitializers()`, so the verified deployed instance is not exposed to first-caller initialization takeover.

title: Missing nonReentrant on targetedPurchase allows reentrancy
reason: The report does not show a concrete reentrant asset-drain path. The function’s external calls are to the configured Rugged token/router, and the claimed callback mechanism is unsupported by the code shown.

title: No slippage protection in swap execution enables sandwich attacks
reason: Slippage bounds belong in the user-supplied Universal Router calldata. This contract also enforces a minimum Rugged amount sufficient to fund the purchase, so the claimed issue is not an inherent protocol flaw here.

title: Inverted validation logic in stake allows minimal stake amount
reason: `stake()` intentionally accepts amounts above `10_000` and rejects values in the NFT-ID range. That is consistent with the hybrid token design implied elsewhere in the contract, not a vulnerability by itself.

title: Incorrect balance check causes DoS and potential fund loss
reason: `afterSwapBalance - beforeSwapBalance` correctly measures the swap’s net Rugged inflow even when the contract already has an existing Rugged balance. The candidate’s stated failure mode is incorrect.

title: No refund on swap failure causes permanent fund loss
reason: If `UNIVERSAL_ROUTER.execute` reverts, the whole transaction reverts, including the ETH transfer. The real issue is only successful-call leftover ETH becoming trapped, which is already retained separately.

title: Incentive rewards not validated before recording
reason: This is not a distinct finding from the broader unchecked-transfer/accounting issue already retained; the same root cause affects incentives, staking, purchases, and payouts.

title: Missing initializer on immutable variable declaration
reason: The verified contract compiled and deployed successfully, and the immutable declaration/assignment pattern used here is valid Solidity.

title: stakeNFTs allows double-staking of same NFT tokenIds
reason: After the first transfer, the NFT is owned by the market contract, so the original user cannot simply stake the same token ID again unless the token implementation is already broken in a broader way.

title: Non-standard IRugged interface lacks safe transfer handling
reason: As written, this is too generic to stand alone. The concrete protocol risk from unchecked Rugged transfer semantics is already captured in the retained accounting finding.

title: executeSwap reverts without error message for zero value
reason: This is developer-experience feedback, not a protocol-level security issue.

title: No deadline validation on swap parameters
reason: The Universal Router enforces the supplied deadline; duplicating that check locally would not change protocol safety.

title: Inconsistent ether unit conversion in stakeNFTs
reason: This describes confusing accounting conventions, not a standalone exploit or realistic protocol-harm issue.
